graphdb_rdf.metric_question: An insurance company deploys an AI system for health insurance risk assessment and pricing for individual natural persons. Identify: its classification, the applicable high-risk requirements, the deployer's fundamental rights obligations, the financial sector supervisory integration, and any exceptions for fraud detection AI.
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: Based on the information retrieved from the knowledge graph regarding the deployment of an AI system for health insurance risk assessment and pricing, here are the relevant details: ### 1. Classification The AI system used by the insurance company for health insurance risk assessment and pricing falls under the category of **High-risk AI systems**. This classification is applicable as it involves assessing risks to health and safety, as well as fundamental rights. ### 2. Applicable High-Risk Requirements The AI system must comply with the **mandatory requirements for high-risk AI systems** as defined in the EU AI Act. These requirements include obligations related to transparency, risk management, and ensuring that the AI system does not infringe on fundamental rights. Specific requirements can be found under the entity [mandatory requirements for high-risk AI systems](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Requirement/mandatory_requirements_for_high-risk_AI_systems). ### 3. Deployer's Fundamental Rights Obligations The deployer of the AI system is obligated to develop and use the AI technology in accordance with **fundamental rights obligations**. This involves ensuring that the deployment does not lead to infringements of obligations under Union law intended to protect fundamental rights. More details can be found under the entity [fundamental rights obligations](https://euaiact.eu/resource/LegalInstrument/fundamental_rights_obligations). ### 4. Financial Sector Supervisory Integration The AI system's deployment must align with the activities of **independent supervisory authorities** that monitor compliance with applicable regulations. In the financial sector, this includes the **European Central Bank** and the **Single Supervisory Mechanism**, which oversee prudential supervisory tasks related to financial institutions and their operations involving AI systems. ### 5. Exceptions for Fraud Detection AI There are specific **exceptions provided to free and open-source AI components** that may apply to AI systems used for fraud detection. The AI system may be designed to raise the risk of fraud detection, which is recognized as a legitimate purpose under the EU AI Act. More information can be found under the entity [detecting fraud](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Concept/detecting_fraud). In summary, the AI system for health insurance risk assessment and pricing is classified as a high-risk AI system, subject to mandatory requirements, fundamental rights obligations, and must integrate with financial sector supervisory frameworks, while also having provisions for exceptions in the context of fraud detection.
